title: QUIC Connection Runtime Deadline Scheduling Architecture
purpose: Describe the shard-owned deadline scheduler that keeps runtime timer work ordered by absolute monotonic due ticks while letting the runtime emit explicit arm and cancel effects.
design_summary:
The deadline scheduler stays shard-owned so timer work is serialized alongside the other connection events that the shard already owns.
Runtime transitions emit explicit timer arm and cancel effects that carry a generation number and a monotonic due tick priority.
The scheduler uses a priority queue keyed by due ticks and sequence, then discards stale registrations by comparing the stored generation and runtime handle before a timer event is re-entered into the connection inbox.
risks: A stale arm effect must never overwrite a newer registration, otherwise an already-superseded deadline could fire again.
Timer effects must stay on the shard owner path so timer work does not become a separate concurrent owner of connection state.
The scheduler must keep monotonic due ticks and sequence ordering aligned with the runtime's timer-state helpers.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scheduling Model
supplemental_sections.content:
Each shard owns one deadline scheduler.
The scheduler stores absolute due ticks and the generation carried by the runtime's timer effects.
When a deadline becomes due, the scheduler converts it back into a timer-expired event and re-enters the owning runtime through the same serialized inbox.

supplemental_sections.heading: Effect Contract
supplemental_sections.content:
Timer arm effects update or replace the current registration for the same handle and timer kind.
Timer cancel effects invalidate the active registration for that handle and timer kind.
The runtime treats a stale timer expiry generation as a no-op.
